articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

images.name: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot
images.caption: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot

articles.headline: Martínez: Cristiano Ronaldo sets the example for Portugal

article.headline: Netherlands rout Scotland 4-0 in Amsterdam friendly
article.description: The Netherlands shrugged off a slow start to go on and register an emphatic 4-0 victory over Scotland in their friendly international at the Johan Cruyff Arena on Friday, finishing strongly in a morale-boosting triumph.
article.linkText: Netherlands rout Scotland 4-0 with late salvo

images.name: Netherlands celeb vs. Scotland_20240322 [1296x729]
images.alt: Wout Weghorst of Holland celebrates the 3-0 during the friendly Interland match between the Netherlands and Scotland at the Johan Cruijff ArenA on March 22, 2024 in Amsterdam, the Netherlands.

article.story:
The Netherlands shrugged off a slow start to go on and register an emphatic 4-0 victory over Scotland in their friendly international at the Johan Cruyff Arena on Friday, finishing strongly in a morale-boosting triumph.

Tijjani Reijnders blasted home from outside the box in the 40th minute to set the Dutch on their way to a fourth successive win.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, NWSL more (U.S.)

But they had to cling onto their lead, with Scotland twice hitting the woodwork, before 33-year-old Georginio Wijnaldum doubled the tally in the 72nd minute with a header from close range.

Substitutes Wout Weghorst and Donyell Malen added two more in the last six minutes as the home team finished strongly. 

Both countries were using the game to prepare for this June's European Championship where the Dutch compete in Group D while Scotland feature in the opening game against hosts Germany.

The Scots had the game's first chance, in the 18th minute, when Ryan Christie ghosted late into the Dutch box, throwing himself at a cross from Billy Gilmour and powering a header at goal that home keeper Mark Flekken got a strong hand to and was able to deflect onto the crossbar.

There were also opportunities for Scott McTominay and Gilmour early in the second half, both denied by blocks from the Dutch defence, while Christie sent a header wide in the 51st minute as John McGinn found him unmarked in front of goal.

Scotland should have been level in the 61st minute when a defensive slip saw Lawrence Shankland steal away possession but, with only the goalkeeper to beat, he missed the target as he hit the crossbar instead.

At the other end Scotland goalkeeper Angus Gunn made a one handed save to deny the injury-prone Memphis Depay, on his return to the Dutch side for the first time since last March.

But Gunn had no answer to Wijnaldum's header and the late efforts from Weghorst and Malen.

Weghorst rose well to head home in the 84th minute and Malen was on the mark just two minutes later as the Dutch sprung to life in the closing stages and might have won by an even bigger margin.

But captain Virgil van Dijk was critical of the performance.

"We need to be more clinical in possession. There are a lot of things we need to do better if we want to do well at the Euros."

Euro 2024 preparations for both countries continue on Tuesday with the Netherlands taking on Germany in Frankfurt while Scotland will be home to Northern Ireland at Hampden Park.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Scott McTominay (Scotland) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Memphis Depay (Netherlands) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
John McGinn (Scotland) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Netherlands 1, Scotland 0. Tijjani Reijnders (Netherlands) right footed shot from outside the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Cody Gakpo.
Substitution, Netherlands. Joey Veerman replaces Mats Wieffer.
Substitution, Netherlands. Denzel Dumfries replaces Jeremie Frimpong.
Substitution, Scotland. Ché Adams replaces Lawrence Shankland.
Substitution, Scotland. John Souttar replaces Kieran Tierney.
Substitution, Scotland. Lewis Ferguson replaces Billy Gilmour.
Goal!  Netherlands 2, Scotland 0. Georginio Wijnaldum (Netherlands) header from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Cody Gakpo with a cross.
Substitution, Scotland. Anthony Ralston replaces Nathan Patterson.
Substitution, Scotland. Stuart Armstrong replaces Ryan Christie.
Substitution, Netherlands. Wout Weghorst replaces Memphis Depay.
Substitution, Netherlands. Donyell Malen replaces Xavi Simons.
Substitution, Netherlands. Teun Koopmeiners replaces Georginio Wijnaldum.
Substitution, Netherlands. Daley Blind replaces Cody Gakpo.
Goal!  Netherlands 3, Scotland 0. Wout Weghorst (Netherlands) header from very close range to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Joey Veerman with a cross   following a corner.
Substitution, Scotland. Kenny McLean replaces John McGinn.
Goal!  Netherlands 4, Scotland 0. Donyell Malen (Netherlands) right footed shot from the left side of the six yard box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Tijjani Reijnders with a through ball.
